title: Mexican Creamy Monsoon Chuck
description: A low-carb-friendly monsoon dish featuring tender chuck roast slow-cooked in a creamy sauce that’s perfect for a comforting dinner.
instructions: Trim any excess fat from the chuck roast and cut into bite-sized pieces.
In a large pot, heat vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add chuck to the pot, season with salt, pepper, chili powder, cumin, and cook until browned on all sides, about 8-10 minutes.
Stir in tomato sauce and heavy cream. Bring to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 2 hours or until the meat is very tender (internal temperature should reach 145°F).
Add chopped bell peppers and onions during the last hour of cooking, allowing them to wilt slightly but not fully.
Serve hot as a main course with optional sides such as quinoa or green salad.
